center.name: 開曼群島
center.summary: 開曼群島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联12种鸟类，包括Caribbean dove、White-crowned pigeon、北撲翅鴷等12种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: barbatulus: breeds in southern Florida, Bahamas, Cuba, and Little Cayman and Cayman Brac in the Cayman Islands; winters in the Amazon Basin *V

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Belize, the Cayman Islands, Colombia (San Andrés island), Honduras (Bay Islands), Jamaica, and Mexico (Yucatán Peninsula)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The loggerhead kingbird is found in throughout the northern West Indies, in the Bahamas, Cayman Islands, Cuba, Isla de la Juventud, Hispaniola (the Dominican Republic and Haiti), Jamaica and Puerto Rico. It very rarely occurs in southern Florida as a vagrant.{{cite web |url=https://www.birds-of-north-america.net/Loggerhead_Kingbird.html#:~:text=The%20Loggerhead%20Kingbird%20is%20a,but%20displays%20a%20bulkier%20body

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: alleni: the Cayman Islands The species has also been recorded several times in Florida. The thick-billed vireo inhabits deciduous woodlands, scrublands, and mangroves

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the Cayman Islands and on the Swan Islands in Honduras

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The species is found in southeastern Florida and the western Caribbean (Cozumel, the Cayman Islands, Cuba, the Bahamas and the Turks and Caicos Islands)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in smaller numbers in Hispaniola (the Dominican Republic and Haiti), Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, the Cayman Islands, Anguilla and other Caribbean islands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Northern rough-winged swallow photographed in central Maine, the northeastern limit of the species' breeding range. The northern rough-winged swallow is native to Bahamas, Belize, Canada, Cayman Islands, Costa Rica, Cuba, El Salvador, Guatemala, Haiti, Honduras, Jamaica, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Puerto Rico, Saint Pierre and Miquelon, Turks and Caicos Islands, and the United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to most of North America, parts of Central America, Cuba, and the Cayman Islands, and is one of the few woodpecker species that migrate

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: leucocephala in Cuba | genus = Amazona | species = leucocephala | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) | synonyms = Psittacus leucocephalus | range_map = Amazona leucocephala map.svg | range_map_caption = Range in green }} The Cuban amazon (Amazona leucocephala), also known as the Cuban parrot and the rose-throated parrot, is a medium-sized mainly green parrot found in woodlands and dry forests of Cuba, the Bahamas and Cayman Islands in the Caribbean. Although they have been observed in the wild in Puerto Rico, they are probably the result of escaped pets, and no reproduction has been recorded.{{cite journal |last1=Falcón |first1=Wilfredo |last2=Tremblay |first2=Raymond L

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|220x220px|Fighting, Madagascar The common myna is native to Asia, with its initial home range spanning Iran, Pakistan, India, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh and Sri Lanka, Afghanistan, Uzbekistan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Myanmar, Malaysia, Singapore, peninsular Thailand, Indochina, Japan (both mainland Japan and the Ryukyu Islands) and China. thumb|left|A common myna as an invasive species in Gülhane Park, Turkey thumb|Visiting a home garden in Colombo, [[Sri Lanka]]The common myna has been introduced to many other parts of the world such as Canada, Australia, Turkey, Israel, New Zealand, New Caledonia, Fiji, the United States (South Florida only), South Africa, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Cayman Islands, Islands in the Indian Ocean (the Seychelles – from which it was subsequently eradicated at great expense,{{Cite journal| journal= PLOS ONE|first1=Thomas|last1=Evans|first2=Elena|last2=Angulo|first3=Corey J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the Bahamas, the Cayman Islands and Cuba.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/woodpeckers/ |title=Woodpeckers |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 12.2 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F
